reasoning: Response surfaces Tajima Beef (GI #2) and Kobe Beef (GI #3) with official grading criteria and descriptions. Intent matched perfectly. Minor issue: 195 items returned and subsequent items (soy, fugu) are off-topic noise diluting focus.
missing_data: No tasting venues or restaurants where visitors can try Tajima beef. No information on how to distinguish Tajima beef vs Kobe beef when dining.
improvement_hint: Add a keyword filter so 'tajima beef' queries surface only beef-related GI records, not all 195 Hyogo food items.

reasoning: Arabic-language response correctly surfaces Tajima Beef and Kobe Beef GI entries with fluent Arabic translations. Same structural noise (unrelated items) as L1-01 but core intent fully met. Translation quality appears high-confidence.
missing_data: No dining venue info in Arabic for Tajima/Kobe beef restaurants.
improvement_hint: Support keyword filtering within prefectural food tool calls to surface topic-relevant items first when a specific product is queried.

reasoning: Query explicitly asks about Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields, not Uji. Tool returned get_japan_heritage for Kyoto which yields Nijo Castle, Toji Temple, Nanzenji — generic Kyoto landmarks, not Minami-Yamashiro or tea fields. Constraint to exclude Uji and focus on the specific village was completely ignored.
missing_data: Minami-Yamashiro village tea cultivation info, Yamashiro-cha GI data, green tea production area details for the specific village.
wrong_data: Top heritage results (Nijo Castle, Toji, Nanzenji) are irrelevant to query. No connection to tea fields.
improvement_hint: Route tea field queries to get_local_food or get_local_specialty with keyword='茶' and filter to specific municipality. get_japan_heritage alone is wrong tool for this intent.

reasoning: Correctly surfaces Yoshida Fire Festival (吉田の火祭) in Fujiyoshida with designation as Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. Date field is null — major gap for planning. Second item (Shinmei fireworks) is loosely related. National heritage section lists non-Yamanashi festivals which dilutes relevance.
missing_data: Festival date (August 26-27 annually) is missing (date: null). No practical info on location, viewing spots, or access from Fuji area.
improvement_hint: Populate date fields for well-known annual festivals. Prune national_heritage section to focus on prefecture-relevant items when query is geographically specific.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about 中芸地域 (Nakayi region) yuzu. Tool returned all 651 Kochi food items, truncated at 500. First matching item is 物部ゆず (a different yuzu GI from Kami city, not Nakayi). Scraped items include tourist spots, travel articles — noise. No 中芸ゆず GI record appeared in visible portion.
missing_data: 中芸ゆず specific GI record. Nakayi region yuzu production details, the 5-village cooperative context.
wrong_data: Scraped items like temples and travel itinerary articles have wrong category (regional_dish) and are irrelevant noise.
improvement_hint: Add keyword routing: query for '中芸ゆず' should filter by keyword before returning 651-item dump. Prefecture-only call with no keyword filter fails niche geographic queries.

reasoning: Query in Chinese about Hirosaki Park cherry blossoms. Response correctly returns Hirosaki Castle (nihon_100_meijo, sakura_meisho_100) and Hirosaki Park (sakura_meisho_100) with coordinates, opening hours, website. Cherry blossom context implicit via kinds tags. Good specificity for planning.
missing_data: Bloom season dates not provided. No description specifically about the cherry blossoms in Chinese.
improvement_hint: Add sakura bloom season to spots with sakura_meisho_100 kind. Multilingual descriptions for Hirosaki Park cherry blossom experience would improve usefulness for Chinese speakers.

reasoning: Korean query about Izumo Taisha. Response correctly returns the shrine as top result with National Treasure designation, coordinates, opening hours, free entry, website. Honden also included. A train station misclassified as shinto_shrine is a minor quality issue. 88 results with relevant matches.
missing_data: Korean-language description of the shrine's significance and marriage/en-musubi prayers is missing.
wrong_data: Izumo Taisha-mae Station tagged with kind 'shinto_shrine' is incorrect categorization.
improvement_hint: Fix station kind tag. Add Korean descriptions for top shrines. The response adequately serves the intent despite minor noise in lower results.

reasoning: French query about Itsukushima Shrine. Tool used lang=en despite French query — minor mismatch but data is correct. Itsukushima Island and Shrine both returned with UNESCO WHS, coordinates, website. Otorii gate also included. Rich heritage designations. Response is well-structured for agent use.
missing_data: No French-language descriptions. Ferry access from Hiroshima mainland not mentioned.
improvement_hint: Pass lang=fr when user query is in French. French descriptions would improve end-user experience significantly.

reasoning: German query about Kumano Kodo. Tool used get_japan_heritage which shows a scope note warning that pilgrimage routes should use search_area or get_traditional_arts instead. Kumano Kodo and Kumano Sanzan do appear in top_canonical_heritage, but buried after Koyasan and Negoro-ji. Tool scope mismatch reduces reliability.
missing_data: Route details for the five Kumano Kodo trails, specific access points, recommended sections. No German-language descriptions.
improvement_hint: For pilgrimage route queries, route to search_area({'q':'熊野古道'}) as the scope note advises. get_japan_heritage correctly warns this but the agent would need to follow through.

reasoning: Russian query about Himeji Castle. Top result is Himeji Castle with full detail: coordinates, opening hours, phone, website, fee, UNESCO WHS, National Treasure, price_band=low, crowd_band=high. Excellent for trip planning. Additional tower structures also included for completeness.
missing_data: No Russian-language description of the castle history or White Heron nickname.
improvement_hint: Add Russian descriptions for top UNESCO sites. Tool response is otherwise excellent with all practical planning fields populated.

reasoning: Thai query about Bunraku in Osaka. Bunraku returned as first result in Thai with UNESCO ICH designation and inscription year 2003. However tool is get_traditional_arts with no prefecture filter — returns 183 items nationally, no Osaka-specific venue information. Practical usefulness for visiting Osaka performance limited.
missing_data: National Bunraku Theater (国立文楽劇場) venue in Osaka, performance schedule, ticket info. Query asked about Osaka specifically.
improvement_hint: Supplement traditional arts results with get_spots for venue lookup. A combined result showing the theater location would make this actionable for the traveler.

reasoning: Vietnamese query about Yumihama Kasuri textile in Tottori. Tool returned get_local_specialty with 7 items — all MAFF GI food products (rakkyo, broccoli, persimmon, watermelon). No Yumihama Kasuri (弓浜絣) craft item appeared. METI craft data for Tottori appears missing from the response.
missing_data: Yumihama Kasuri (弓浜絣) METI traditional craft designation entirely absent. Tottori craft specialists or workshops.
improvement_hint: Verify Yumihama Kasuri is in METI densan dataset. If present, check category filter routing for craft items in Tottori. Missing key item for the explicit query.

reasoning: Tagalog query about Naoshima Art Island. Response returns Benesse Art Site (museum, tier:must_see), Naoshima New Museum, municipality, and scraped tourism info in Chinese/Japanese. Core content relevant and grounded. Scrape items in Chinese with nav-chrome-like content reduce expression quality.
missing_data: Chichu Art Museum, Yayoi Kusama pumpkin sculptures not listed. Island access info (ferry from Uno or Takamatsu) absent.
improvement_hint: Chichu Art Museum should be in Wikidata data for Naoshima. Scraped items' multilingual content (Chinese tourism pages) adds noise — filter by relevance or flag clearly.

reasoning: English query about Kakunodate samurai district. Top result is Kakunodate samurai district (武家屋敷) with detailed description, coordinates, heritage designation, typical_visit_minutes=120, weeping cherry blossoms noted in description. Cherry bark museum also included. Excellent comprehensiveness and practical detail.
improvement_hint: Near-perfect response. Could add opening hours for individual samurai houses and seasonal notes for cherry blossom viewing period (late April).

reasoning: Indonesian query about Onomichi. Response returns Shimanami Kaido (cycling route, prominence_score=1), art museum, and Onomichi city with Japan Heritage designation. Good coverage of key attraction. Onomichi Castle tagged as museum kind is slightly incorrect. Some description fields empty.
missing_data: Onomichi's iconic temple walk (山手の小道), cat alley (猫の細道), and old townscape character not captured. Historic street atmosphere underrepresented.
wrong_data: Onomichi Castle tagged with kind 'museum' is inaccurate — it is a reconstruction that now houses a museum but is primarily known as a castle site.
improvement_hint: Add scraped municipal content for Onomichi's temple trail and hillside character. The cycling route prominence overshadows the town's walkable historic charm.

reasoning: Spanish query about Nachi Falls. Top result is Nachi Falls with coordinates, prominence_score=0.95, Natural Monument and Place of Scenic Beauty designations. 48 Waterfalls of Nachi also returned. Nachi Primeval Forest included. One spurious item (Nachi Waterfall painting in Tokyo museum) is slightly noisy.
missing_data: No connection to Kumano Nachi Taisha shrine or Seiganto-ji temple which are integral to the Nachi Falls experience. UNESCO WHS status for the wider Kumano pilgrimage is absent here.
wrong_data: Nachi 滝図 painting (Tokyo museum artifact) appearing in results is off-topic for a waterfall visit query.
improvement_hint: Link Nachi Falls to nearby Kumano Nachi Taisha in result grouping. Filter out artifact records when query is about a geographical feature.

reasoning: Hindi query about Shiretoko Peninsula in Hokkaido. Response correctly returns Shiretoko, Shiretoko Peninsula, Shiretoko National Park all with UNESCO WHS designation. Mount Io and Shiretoko World Heritage Conservation Center also included. Good coverage of the UNESCO protected area.
missing_data: No Hindi-language descriptions. Practical access info (Utoro vs Rausu side, winter road closures) absent.
improvement_hint: Add Hindi descriptions for top UNESCO sites. Seasonal access warnings for Shiretoko (roads close in winter) would significantly improve practical usefulness.

reasoning: Query about Dewa Sanzan in Yamagata. Target item (出羽三山, Three Mountains of Dewa) appears second after an irrelevant result (Tebajima island in Tokushima, 出羽島). The first result has 'dewa' in name but is completely wrong geographically. Search term '出羽' is ambiguous and causes ranking failure.
missing_data: Details on the three individual mountains (Mt. Haguro, Mt. Gas-san, Mt. Yudono), yamabushi ascetic practices, access from Tsuruoka.
wrong_data: First result 出羽島 (Tebajima, Tokushima Prefecture) is completely irrelevant to the Yamagata query.
improvement_hint: Use prefecture filter when query specifies Yamagata. search_area({'q':'出羽三山', 'prefecture':'Yamagata'}) would eliminate the Tokushima false positive.

reasoning: Portuguese query about Yakushima. Top result is Yakushima island with UNESCO WHS, Wilderness Area, Ramsar designations. National park and Jomon Cedar primeval forest area also returned. Strong heritage designation coverage.
missing_data: Jomon Cedar (縄文杉) trekking info, access from Kagoshima ferry, age/size stats for the ancient cedar trees.
improvement_hint: Add Jomon Cedar as a named spot with trail difficulty and typical visit time. The island entry alone undersells the trekking experience that is the primary draw.

reasoning: Korean query about Sado Island. Tool used get_japan_heritage which returns Sado Gold Mine (佐渡金山) as top canonical heritage — relevant but only one aspect. Japan Heritage stories are about Shinano River pottery culture, not Sado Island specifically. The island's ecology, Toki (crested ibis), and cultural landscape underrepresented.
missing_data: Sado Island as a destination: Ondeko (demon drum dance), Noh theater tradition, toki crested ibis sanctuary, kyoto-style culture in remote island context.
improvement_hint: For island queries, route to get_spots with municipality filter or search_area('佐渡島') to capture the full tourism profile beyond just the gold mine heritage angle.

reasoning: Query asks about Tohoku region onsen ryokan. Tool only fetched Akita. Multiple confirmed 日本秘湯を守る会 ryokan returned with addresses. However query spans Tohoku (6 prefectures) not just Akita. No coordinates or phone for most entries limits practical usefulness. Constraint (Tohoku region) only partially honored.
missing_data: Ryokan from other Tohoku prefectures (Iwate, Miyagi, Yamagata, Fukushima, Aomori). Onsen type, coordinates, booking info absent for most entries.
improvement_hint: Expand to region fan-out for Tohoku when region is specified. The hito_yu_kai source lacks coordinates — enrich with geocoding.

reasoning: Query asks for 宿坊 (temple lodging) along the Shikoku pilgrimage. Tool returned hotels for Tokushima which are generic ryokan, guest houses, and regular hotels from OSM. No shukubo/宿坊 temple lodging appears. The constraint (pilgrimage accommodation type) completely missed. None of the 88 temples' lodgings shown.
missing_data: Shukubo (宿坊) temple lodgings along the 88 temple Shikoku pilgrimage. None of the visible results are pilgrimage-specific accommodation.
wrong_data: Results are generic commercial hotels not related to henro pilgrimage lodging.
improvement_hint: Add shukubo as a lodging_type in hotel data. Consider sourcing temple lodging from pilgrimage official sources. This is a high-demand query type that the current data layer cannot serve.

reasoning: Chinese query recommending agricultural farm experience in Hokkaido. Tool returned get_spots for Hokkaido which gives tourist attractions — volcanoes, clock tower, castles. No 農業体験 (agritourism/farm experience) content returned at all. The activity type constraint is completely ignored.
missing_data: Farm stay or agri-experience venues in Hokkaido (lavender farms in Furano, melon farms, dairy experiences). No agritourism data in corpus.
wrong_data: Showa-shinzan (volcano) and Sapporo Clock Tower are irrelevant to farm experience queries.
improvement_hint: Add agritourism experience as a category in scraped spots. Farm experience (農業体験) is a major Hokkaido tourism segment with no data coverage. Consider scraping agricultural tourism associations.

reasoning: English query about traditional pottery in Kyushu. Tool returned Saga crafts: Imari-Arita Ware and Karatsu Ware with official METI descriptions and production areas. Hakata Ori weave also included (multi-prefecture). see_also_wikidata_venues adds Takeo museum. Good coverage of the pottery tradition with official sources.
missing_data: Kiln visit locations, pottery experience workshops, specific town visitor information for Arita/Karatsu.
improvement_hint: Supplement METI craft records with nearby venue spots from get_spots to show where visitors can actually see kilns and try making pottery.

reasoning: Korean query about Tohoku traditional festivals. Tool only fetched Aomori festivals (Nebuta, Hachinohe Sansha Taisai). Tohoku has 6 prefectures with major festivals (Sendai Tanabata, Akita Kanto, Yamagata Hanagasa, Morioka Sansa). Coverage is sparse — only 2 Aomori items in local items. National heritage is non-Tohoku.
missing_data: Sendai Tanabata Festival, Akita Kanto Festival, Yamagata Hanagasa Festival, Morioka Sansa Festival — all Tohoku's most famous festivals absent.
improvement_hint: Enable region fan-out for Tohoku region queries in get_festivals. A single-prefecture call cannot answer a regional question. National heritage section should prioritize Tohoku items when region is specified.
